title: Harden web API against DNS rebinding before exposing terminal
description: The local web server's mutation guard accepts any Origin whose netloc matches the request Host header. A browser can reach 127.0.0.1 through a rebinding/attacker-controlled hostname, send Host and Origin for that hostname, and pass require_same_origin_request. Repro: connect to 127.0.0.1:<port> with Host: attacker.test:<port>, Origin: http://attacker.test:<port>, Content-Type: application/json, POST /api/matters; current server returns 201. Because /api/terminal/sessions starts a shell and /input writes to it, the fix should validate Host/Origin against the actual configured bind host or explicit localhost allowlist, ideally with an unguessable per-server token for all mutation and terminal endpoints. Add regression tests for forged Host+Origin.
close_reason: Hardened API host/origin validation and added forged Host+Origin regression tests

title: Make Cytoscape matters graph visually usable
description: The first Cytoscape migration renders but the dense matter graph is not useful visually. Rework the default graph experience so users can understand and navigate the graph: avoid unusable hairball overviews, provide meaningful focused/default views, and verify visually on small and large example states before closing.
close_reason: Fixed graph usability: large graphs now default to Attention scope, Universe and All graph are explicit scopes, node selection focuses local neighborhoods, layout switches avoid Dagre strips, and visual checks covered large default, Universe, All, focused, and small graph states.

title: Prevent reconciliation from returning cyclic dependency edges
description: The exported reconcile_candidates API returns new_edges without seeing or validating the existing dependency graph. A caller that applies those edges can create cycles that make resolved(), universe(), frontier(), and horizon() raise ValueError. Repro: with existing dependency a -> b, a fake classifier can return link new_before_existing for c vs a and link existing_before_new for c vs b; reconcile_candidates returns [('c','a'), ('b','c')], and existing | new_edges is cyclic. The fix should either accept existing dependencies and reject/drop cycle-forming edges with has_dependency_cycle, or return relationship proposals plus a separate validated apply step. Add an identity regression test for a multi-neighbour cycle.

title: Reject dependency cycles when merging public states
description: Deep review found that merge_public_state validates incoming public dependency endpoints but does not reject cycles. An incoming public state with both a -> b and b -> a is accepted; if both matters otherwise resolve, universe()/resolved() later raises ValueError: dependency cycle. The web add_dependency path already rejects this class with has_dependency_cycle, so sharing should enforce the same invariant and add a regression test in tests/test_sharing.py.
close_reason: Fixed. Added shared has_dependency_cycle engine helper, public merge cycle rejection, regression tests, pytest test extra, README install command, and verified with uv full suite plus a fresh pip editable install.

title: Relationship-aware reconciliation (same/resolves/link)
description: Slice 2. Generalize the candidate decision from merge/new to a role/status-aware 4-way relationship: SAME (merge true duplicate), RESOLVES (new matter flips an existing OPEN matter's satisfied condition truths false->true + adds prereq edge), LINK (complementary distinct -> add dependency edge in LLM-given direction), DISTINCT (nothing). Guards: never SAME across problem/solution or resolved-finding/open-question. Conservative resolves (only named conditions, clear evidence). Classifier as precision gate lets us widen the candidate floor. Wire into Kenett pipeline.
design: Add Relationship, CLASSIFY_SCHEMA/SYSTEM, classify_relationship, reconcile_candidates (returns matters,conditions,id_map,new_edges,flips) to identity.py. Derive existing status from conditions (all true=resolved). Pipeline: reconcile -> apply id_map (deps remap+provenance), append new_edges to pending_deps, use flipped conditions. CANDIDATE_FLOOR=0.55, top_k=3.
acceptance_criteria: classify_relationship returns same/resolves/link/distinct with direction + satisfied condition indices; reconcile_candidates merges SAME, flips named conditions on RESOLVES + adds edge, adds directed edge on LINK, nothing on DISTINCT; problem/solution and resolved/open pairs are not merged; offline tests with a fake classifier cover all four; full pytest green.
close_reason: Delivered: status-aware extraction (resolved/open + per-condition truth), embedding-based identity (model2vec + npz store, match/ingest), relationship-aware reconciliation (same/resolves/link/distinct), and a deterministic role/status merge guard. 75 tests pass; verified on the 10-paper corpus.

title: Embedding-based matter identity (replace slug dedup)
description: Slice 1 of embedding identity + reconciliation. Replace slug first-write-wins dedup with embedding similarity: embed candidate text, find nearest existing matters, auto-merge high-similarity, LLM-adjudicate the middle band, else new. Reusable, offline-testable API in src/matters/identity.py; Kenett pipeline merge step uses it. Local model2vec default (lazy), FakeEmbedder for tests, numpy brute-force nearest, npz sidecar store. Dedup only in slice 1 (no condition changes); reconciliation/cross-paper resolution is slice 2.
design: src/matters/identity.py: Embedder (LocalEmbedder model2vec lazy / FakeEmbedder / get_embedder), EmbeddingStore (npz sidecar + numpy nearest), match_candidate (high=0.90/low=0.78 bands + _llm_same_matter), ingest_candidates (returns id_map). numpy+model2vec hard deps. Pipeline applies id_map to pending_deps + provenance.
acceptance_criteria: Reworded duplicate matters merge to one node; distinct stay separate; middle band escalates to LLM; merges repoint dependencies with no dangling/self-loops; provenance keeps both sources; embeddings persist in a sidecar; full pytest passes offline with a fake embedder; no-embedder falls back to slug without crashing.
close_reason: Delivered: status-aware extraction (resolved/open + per-condition truth), embedding-based identity (model2vec + npz store, match/ingest), relationship-aware reconciliation (same/resolves/link/distinct), and a deterministic role/status merge guard. 75 tests pass; verified on the 10-paper corpus.

title: Serialize state mutations and write state atomically
description: Web mutation handlers load, mutate, and save the JSON state without a shared lock, while ThreadingHTTPServer can execute requests concurrently. A forced two-writer create_matter race lost one of two matters with no error. Protect state mutations with a per-state lock and make save_state use atomic temp-file replacement to avoid lost updates and partial writes.
close_reason: Fixed with API mutation guards, serialized atomic state writes, portable terminal configuration, dependency validation, and regression tests.

title: Reject cross-origin writes to the local web API
description: The local ThreadingHTTPServer accepts mutating requests from any Origin and read_json parses JSON regardless of Content-Type. A cross-origin simple POST with Content-Type: text/plain can create matters or run command/extract side effects without reading the response. Add an Origin/Host/token check and require application/json for API mutation routes; cover POST/PATCH/DELETE and terminal/session routes.
close_reason: Fixed with API mutation guards, serialized atomic state writes, portable terminal configuration, dependency validation, and regression tests.

title: LLM-based matter extraction for scientific papers
description: The current extractor (src/matters/extraction.py) is a regex/marker scanner: on real paper prose with no Goal:/Problem: markers it degrades to an 80-char truncation of the first line. Add an LLM-based semantic engine that reads prose abstracts/sections and extracts claims/contributions as matters, evidence quality as conditions, and methods/prerequisites as dependencies. Augment, do not replace: marker engine stays as offline fallback. LLM engine runs for all source types when an API key exists; falls back to marker on no key/error. Output preserves the existing proposal contract. Model configurable, default claude-sonnet-4-6. Text-only input in v1.
design: New src/matters/llm_extraction.py with llm_extraction_proposal(...,*,client=None,model=None), build_extraction_proposal(...) orchestrator with fallback, _llm_available() gate. Uses client.messages.create with output_config.format json_schema. Reuse slugify/dedupe_candidates from extraction.py. Wire cli.py (add --model/--no-llm) and web.py extract. Add anthropic to pyproject dependencies (hard dep). engine field added to output (additive).
acceptance_criteria: Prose abstract without markers yields multiple specific candidates (not one truncated line); conditions are evidence-grounded not generic; dependency_candidates are semantic with human reasons; proposal contract preserved (source_type/candidates/dependency_candidates/requires_confirmation); no key or API error falls back to marker engine and never crashes; full pytest passes offline via injected fake client.
close_reason: Implemented LLM-based extraction engine (llm_extraction.py) with marker fallback, CLI/web wiring, anthropic dependency, offline tests (48 pass), README + SKILL docs. Independently validated: no critical/important findings.

title: Fix switching to general matters graph in UI
description: Why this exists: switching the running visualization window to the general matters graph path is reported not to work. What needs to be done: reproduce switching to /Users/matthias/.local/share/matters/matters.json, identify whether the failure is backend path handling, frontend form behavior, cached assets, or graph rendering, then fix and verify in browser.
notes: Reproduced on port 8765: existing Python process served updated HTML/JS but POST /api/state returned 404, so the UI was ahead of the backend. Verified fresh server on 8786 switches to /Users/matthias/.local/share/matters/matters.json with 22 matters and 15 dependencies. Fixed client-side non-JSON error handling, added a restart-specific stale-backend message, and cache-busted the module script.
close_reason: Fixed stale-backend graph switch failure reporting and verified fresh server switching to the general matters graph.

title: Replace web chat with terminal drawer
description: Why this exists: the right-side Codex chat is not the interaction model the user wants. What needs to be done: remove the chat panel, add a bottom browser terminal backed by a local zsh session rooted at /Users/matthias/.openclaw/workspace, and verify the graph UI still works.
close_reason: Replaced right-side chat with a bottom xterm.js terminal drawer backed by local zsh in /Users/matthias/.openclaw/workspace; verified tests, compile, live PTY API, and screenshot.

title: Connect chat panel to Codex CLI
description: Why this exists: the web UI chat panel currently runs local matters commands only. The user wants it to run a Codex CLI instance rooted at /Users/matthias/.openclaw/workspace so the chat can interact with agents directly. What needs to be done: design and implement a safe local process bridge from the browser chat panel to Codex CLI, preserve matters commands where useful, and verify process lifecycle behavior.
design: Approved story direction: safer V1 uses codex exec per chat message instead of a long-lived interactive browser PTY. Working directory must be /Users/matthias/.openclaw/workspace.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The chat panel has a clear mode for sending messages to Codex, separate from local matters commands. 2. Codex runs with working directory /Users/matthias/.openclaw/workspace. 3. User messages from the browser are passed to Codex CLI. 4. Codex responses are displayed in the chat panel. 5. The UI shows when Codex is running and prevents accidental double-submits. 6. Errors, timeouts, and nonzero Codex exits are shown clearly in the chat. 7. Existing local matters commands like universe, frontier, horizon, and unlock continue to work. 8. The server does not use --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox. 9. The implementation has tests for the server-side Codex bridge using a fake command. 10. The app still runs via matters web --state.
notes: Research checkpoint: Codex CLI is at /opt/homebrew/bin/codex. Interactive CLI supports --cd; non-interactive path is codex exec --cd /Users/matthias/.openclaw/workspace --json. The web app currently has POST /api/command for local matters commands and no streaming/process lifecycle API. Key design decision: persistent interactive PTY session vs one codex exec job per user message. Safer v1 recommendation is codex exec per message with command allow/deny controls, timeout, transcript display, and no dangerous bypass flags.
close_reason: Done

title: Compact graph component spacing
description: Why this exists: visual inspection shows the force-directed graph still places graph parts too far apart. What needs to be done: tune force layout, camera framing, and possibly add explicit compactness behavior so connected components and clusters sit closer together.
close_reason: Reduced repulsion, tightened link distances, added compacting center force, and visually verified real graph desktop/mobile clustering.

title: Add explicit graph zoom controls
description: Why this exists: the 3D graph supports wheel/trackpad zoom, but the UI does not show visible zoom controls. What needs to be done: add explicit zoom in and zoom out controls to the graph toolbar and wire them to the 3D camera.
close_reason: Added visible zoom in/out toolbar controls wired to the 3D graph camera, with tests and browser smoke verification.

title: Tighten clustered graph styling
description: Why this exists: the force-directed 3D graph currently renders nodes with planet-like rings and the layout spreads nodes too far apart. What needs to be done: remove ring/planet styling, use simpler node markers, and tune force/layout/camera parameters so clusters are more compact.
close_reason: Removed planet ring styling, reduced node and arrow sizes, tightened force layout distances, and replaced zoom-to-fit with stable compact camera framing.

title: Use clustered force-directed 3D graph layout
description: Why this exists: the current 3D graph uses a deterministic sphere/depth layout, but the user wants a PuppyGraph-like clustered force-directed graph with better natural grouping. What needs to be done: replace or augment the custom Three.js layout with a lightweight force-directed 3D graph tool, preserve existing controls and interactions, and verify rendering.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The graph view uses a force-directed clustered 3D layout rather than the previous deterministic sphere/depth layout. 2. It visually resembles the provided PuppyGraph-style reference more closely, with connected nodes naturally clustering. 3. Existing matter status colors, selected state, dependency arrows, labels, filters, inspector, editing controls, and command panel continue to work. 4. Clicking a node selects it and fades unrelated nodes/links into the background. 5. Node dragging does not reposition an individual matter as saved state; grabbing a node should still be usable to rotate/orbit the graph. 6. The UI still runs through matters web --state. 7. WebGL fallback remains clear. 8. Existing tests pass and frontend asset tests cover the force-directed graph dependency.
close_reason: Switched the 3D graph surface to 3d-force-graph with clustered force-directed layout, labels, arrows, filtering, focus fading, WebGL fallback, docs, and tests.

title: Make matters graph view 3D
description: Why this exists: the current browser UI renders the matters graph as a 2D SVG. The user wants the graph view to become 3D while preserving graph operations, editing, filtering, and the command panel. What needs to be done: design and implement a 3D graph visualization for the web UI with camera controls, selection/focus behavior, status styling, and practical fallback/testing.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The main graph view renders matters as 3D nodes and dependencies as 3D directed edges. 2. Users can rotate, pan, and zoom the camera with mouse/trackpad controls. 3. Users can grab a node and drag to rotate the whole graph around all axes, using the node as a handle rather than moving only that node. 4. Node colors/statuses remain clear for resolved, actionable/universe, blocked, and selected matters. 5. Clicking a node selects it and updates the existing inspector. 6. When a node is selected, unrelated nodes and edges fade into the visual background, while directly connected nodes/edges remain prominent. 7. Text search and status filters continue to affect the graph. 8. Existing create matter, condition editing, dependency editing, and command/chat panel workflows continue to work. 9. Graph-view buttons for universe, frontier, horizon, and unlock still work. 10. The 3D view has a reset-camera control. 11. If WebGL is unavailable, the UI shows a clear fallback message instead of a blank graph. 12. The implementation remains easy to run through matters web --state. 13. Existing tests continue to pass, with added tests where practical.
notes: Software-factory checkpoint: user approved revised 3D graph story on 2026-06-01. Key additional interaction: grabbing a node and dragging rotates the whole graph around all axes; it must not drag only the individual node.
close_reason: Implemented Three.js 3D graph view with WebGL fallback, node-handle graph rotation across axes, camera controls, status styling, focus fading, docs, and tests.

title: Build browser-based matters graph UI
description: Why this exists: matters.global currently exposes graph operations through CLI/text outputs and has only a decorative docs canvas, not an actual browser UI for interacting with saved matters graphs. What needs to be done: design and implement a web-based GUI that renders the real matters graph with nodes and edges, supports zooming/filtering, lets users create matters/conditions/dependencies, and provides a chat/control panel for agent-assisted operations such as universe, frontier, horizon, unlock, extraction, and other matters workflows.
design: Approved story adjustments: node selection must fade non-connected graph elements into the visual background; common operations must be available in both the chat/control panel and graph interface; v1 should be a local web server included in the Python package, e.g. matters web.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The app launches locally via a packaged command such as matters web and opens in a browser. 2. The main view renders the real matters JSON state as graph nodes and directed dependency edges. 3. The graph supports pan and zoom. 4. Nodes visually distinguish resolved, actionable/universe, blocked, and selected states. 5. The UI filters by status and text search. 6. Selecting a node shows id, conditions, prerequisites, dependents, derived status, and fades non-connected nodes/edges into the visual background. 7. Users can create a matter with one or more conditions. 8. Users can add, edit, and toggle condition truth values. 9. Users can create and remove dependencies between existing matters. 10. Universe, frontier, horizon, unlock, and basic create/extract-style operations are available from both the chat/control panel and graph view controls. 11. V1 chat may be a local command interface backed by the matters engine; direct Codex/Claude adapter integration can follow once a stable local agent API is available. 12. Changes persist to the same JSON state format used by the CLI. 13. Existing CLI and tests continue to work. 14. Docs explain how to run the web UI.
notes: Software-factory checkpoint: user approved updated story on 2026-06-01. Key approved requirements: local browser UI via matters web; real graph rendering; pan/zoom/filter; node selection inspector; fade non-connected nodes/edges on selection; create matters/conditions/dependencies; chat/control panel plus graph-view controls for universe/frontier/horizon/unlock/basic create/extract operations; persist to existing JSON state; keep CLI/tests working.
close_reason: Implemented matters web local graph UI with API, SVG graph, filters, editing controls, command panel, docs, and tests.

title: Remove Public demo hero badge
description: Remove the 'Public demo' hero status badge from the matters.global public site hero section.
acceptance_criteria: The hero no longer renders the Public demo/next visible step status badge; layout remains valid.
notes: Removed the hero status badge containing 'Public demo' and 'next visible step' from docs/index.html. Removed the now-unused .hero-status CSS rules and responsive overrides from docs/assets/styles.css. Verification: static smoke check confirmed those strings/classes are gone; pytest passed with 25 tests.
close_reason: Removed Public demo hero badge

title: Update personal matters graph after website work
description: Update /Users/matthias/.local/share/matters/matters.json to reflect completed matters.global website and quick-start work from this session.
acceptance_criteria: Relevant conditions in the explicit matters state file are updated based on verified completed work, without changing unrelated matters.
notes: Updated /Users/matthias/.local/share/matters/matters.json based on verified public matters.global site work. Marked true: publish_matters_global_system / First public demo or walkthrough is published; develop_matters_global_public_story / Core narrative is articulated; develop_matters_global_public_story / At least one short public version of the story is published. Left false: People outside the project have been invited to try it, because no invitations were verified. Validation: json.tool passed; matters universe and unlock loaded the state successfully.
close_reason: Updated explicit matters state with verified website/story progress

title: Replace technical Quick Start state note
description: Replace the technical local-state note in the matters.global Quick Start with user-facing copy about starting messy and letting the agent propose matters.
acceptance_criteria: Quick Start no longer mentions the default local state path in the note; it uses the approved 'Start messy...' wording.
notes: Replaced the technical default-state note in docs/index.html with the approved user-facing copy: 'Start messy. Tell the agent what you are trying to understand or move forward...' Verification: HTML smoke check passed and pytest passed with 25 tests.
close_reason: Quick Start note replaced with approved user-facing copy

title: Revise Quick Start for agent-first workflow
description: Update matters.global Quick Start so it tells users to install matters.global, start an agent such as Codex or Claude, and use the system through the agent workflow instead of presenting the CLI as the primary path.
acceptance_criteria: Quick Start on the public site clearly presents the AI/agent route first, with install, agent start, and natural-language use examples; CLI commands remain available only as supporting context if useful.
notes: Revised docs/index.html Quick Start from CLI-first to agent-first. It now tells users to install matters.global, start Codex or Claude in a workspace, and use natural-language prompts to create/review matters, inspect actionable work, and extract candidates while asking before saving. CLI commands remain as supporting context for direct inspection. Verification: HTML parser smoke check passed, local HTTP server served revised copy, pytest passed with 25 tests.
close_reason: Quick Start revised for agent-first workflow

title: Add Quick Start guide to matters.global
description: Explain on matters.global how a new person can get started with the Matters tool.
acceptance_criteria: The public site includes a visible Quick Start section or page with install/setup, first state file, creating matters, inspecting universe, and next-step commands.
notes: Added a visible Quick Start section to docs/index.html and linked it from the hero and primary navigation. The section covers install, resolved state path, creating a first dependency chain, universe/unlock, explicit state files, and extraction from notes. Added responsive CSS for command blocks and quick-start cards. Verification: local HTTP server returned index and stylesheet successfully; HTML parser smoke check found required quick-start commands; pytest passed with 25 tests.
close_reason: Quick Start section added to public site

title: Improve extraction skill for creativity research graph building
description: Improve the extraction skill or underlying matters CLI so creativity research sources yield useful candidate matters, observable conditions, and dependency proposals.
acceptance_criteria: Extraction results on the test corpus are materially better against the documented expectations, and automated or reproducible checks cover the changed behavior.
notes: Improved extractor behavior for creativity research corpus. Added speaker-prefixed marker parsing for conversation lines such as 'Agent: Goal: ...'. Added creativity/research-specific condition labels covering source context, evidence quality/limits, review/persistence, research gaps, transfer conditions, and graph impact. Reduced dependency proposal noise by ignoring generic overlap words before proposing name-overlap dependencies. Added regression tests for speaker-prefixed markers, domain-specific conditions, and dependency-noise reduction. Verification: uv run --with pytest --with-editable . pytest => 25 passed. Manual corpus check: conversation fixture now extracts 4 marker candidates instead of falling back to the first unstructured line.
close_reason: Improved extraction behavior and covered it with tests

title: Test current extraction skill on creativity research sources
description: Run the current extraction skill/CLI against the creativity research corpus and record where candidate matters, conditions, deduplication, or dependency proposals are weak.
acceptance_criteria: Test output is captured; specific failure modes and improvement targets are recorded as notes or follow-up Beads.
notes: Baseline run completed against examples/creativity_research/ using /Users/matthias/.local/share/matters/matters.json. research_notes extracted 6 candidates but with generic placeholder conditions and many noisy name-overlap dependencies. conversation extracted only the first unstructured line and missed speaker-prefixed embedded markers like 'Agent: Goal: ...'. paper_excerpt extracted 5 candidates but also produced generic conditions and noisy dependencies. Current tests still pass: uv run --with pytest --with-editable . pytest => 22 passed. Improvement targets: parse speaker-prefixed markers, produce domain-aware condition labels for creativity/research matters, reduce dependency noise by ignoring generic overlap words and scoring meaningful terms, and align IDs with expected.json where possible.
close_reason: Baseline extraction behavior captured against creativity research corpus

title: Define creativity research extraction test corpus
description: Select representative source material for creativity research extraction: notes, papers, conversations, URLs or markdown exports.
acceptance_criteria: A small test corpus exists or is referenced, with expected high-level matters, condition quality expectations, and dependency-review expectations.
notes: Defined creativity research extraction corpus under examples/creativity_research/: research_notes.txt, conversation.txt, paper_excerpt.txt, and expected.json. The expected file records high-level target matters, condition quality expectations, dependency-review expectations, and known baseline gaps. JSON fixture validates with jq.
close_reason: Creativity research extraction corpus and expected outcomes added

title: Harden extraction skill for creativity research
description: Test and improve the matters extraction workflow enough to start building a useful matters graph for creativity research.
acceptance_criteria: The extraction skill has representative creativity-research tests; identified extraction gaps are fixed or tracked; the workflow can produce reviewed matters and dependency candidates for a creativity-research graph.
notes: Extraction track completed through a reviewable graph proposal. Added corpus fixtures, captured baseline gaps, improved extraction behavior, added tests, and exported examples/creativity_research/initial_graph_proposal.json for explicit acceptance before live-state persistence.
close_reason: Extraction workflow hardened and initial creativity graph proposal exported

title: Verify matters.global website delivery
description: Verify end-to-end that matters.global serves the intended website over HTTPS after DNS propagation.
acceptance_criteria: matters.global loads the intended site over HTTPS; certificate is valid; redirect/canonical behavior for www vs apex is recorded.
notes:
Verification after DNS fix: authoritative UDAG and public resolvers return the corrected records. HTTP apex serves GitHub Pages. HTTPS apex reaches GitHub Pages but certificate is not yet valid for matters.global. GitHub Pages API reports https_enforced=false; attempting PUT /repos/matthiasroder/matters.global/pages with https_enforced=true returned 'The certificate does not exist yet' (HTTP 404). Local resolver still had no www response at check time, while public resolvers had the correct CNAME. Next step: wait for DNS/cache/certificate provisioning, then rerun verification and enable HTTPS enforcement.
Retry to enable HTTPS on 2026-05-31: GitHub Pages API still reports https_certificate=null and https_enforced=false. PUT /repos/matthiasroder/matters.global/pages with https_enforced=true returned 'The certificate does not exist yet' (HTTP 404). Public resolvers 1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8, and 9.9.9.9 resolve www.matters.global CNAME matthiasroder.github.io and GitHub Pages A records. HTTPS cannot be enforced until GitHub creates the certificate.
2026-06-01 HTTPS check: GitHub Pages still reports https_certificate=null and https_enforced=false. DNS remains correct: apex A/AAAA records point to GitHub Pages; www.matters.global CNAME points to matthiasroder.github.io; no apex CAA record blocks issuance. HTTPS requests still fail certificate hostname validation. Triggered a Pages rebuild via POST /pages/builds; build completed successfully. Retried PUT /pages with https_enforced=true after rebuild and GitHub still returned 'The certificate does not exist yet' (HTTP 404). Re-saved cname=matters.global through the Pages API to nudge certificate provisioning; certificate remained null immediately after.
close_reason: HTTPS certificate approved, enforcement enabled, apex and www verified

title: Fix matters.global DNS for GitHub Pages
description: Apply the DNS and GitHub Pages configuration changes needed for matters.global to serve the website.
acceptance_criteria: Required A/AAAA/CNAME records and GitHub Pages custom-domain configuration are corrected, with no conflicting records left in place.
notes:
Requires DNS provider access for matters.global, apparently served by United Domains nameservers ns.udag.net/ns.udag.org/ns.udag.de. Apply these DNS changes: keep apex A records 185.199.108.153, 185.199.109.153, 185.199.110.153, 185.199.111.153; replace the stale apex AAAA 2001:1a50:11:0:9c77:dff:fea4:2e8d with GitHub Pages AAAA records 2606:50c0:8000::153, 2606:50c0:8001::153, 2606:50c0:8002::153, 2606:50c0:8003::153; add CNAME www.matters.global -> matthiasroder.github.io. After propagation, GitHub Pages HTTPS can be enabled.
Verified after user update. Authoritative UDAG nameservers now return correct apex A records, correct GitHub Pages apex AAAA records, and www.matters.global CNAME matthiasroder.github.io. Public resolvers 1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8, and 9.9.9.9 also return the new www CNAME and GitHub AAAA records. Local resolver still had stale/no www response at verification time, consistent with resolver cache propagation.
close_reason: DNS records corrected and verified on authoritative nameservers and public resolvers

title: Audit matters.global GitHub Pages and DNS configuration
description: Inspect the current GitHub Pages settings, repository Pages configuration, CNAME file if any, and DNS records for matters.global.
acceptance_criteria: Current repo Pages target, custom-domain setting, CNAME state, registrar/DNS records, and observed DNS/HTTP behavior are documented in the issue notes.
notes: Audit completed 2026-05-31. Repo is public matthiasroder/matters.global. GitHub Pages API reports status=built, custom domain cname=matters.global, source=main:/docs, build_type=legacy, public=true, https_enforced=false. Local Pages source has docs/CNAME containing exactly 'matters.global' and docs/index.html exists. DNS authority is ns.udag.net/ns.udag.org/ns.udag.de. Apex A records are correct for GitHub Pages: 185.199.108.153, 185.199.109.153, 185.199.110.153, 185.199.111.153. Apex AAAA is wrong/stale: 2001:1a50:11:0:9c77:dff:fea4:2e8d, which serves the old Uberspace/nginx WordPress site. www.matters.global has no A, AAAA, or CNAME records. HTTP behavior confirms split routing: curl -4 http://matters.global returns GitHub Pages; curl -6 http/https returns Uberspace; curl -4 https://matters.global reaches GitHub Pages but certificate is not valid yet; www does not resolve. Required DNS fix: replace/remove stale apex AAAA and use GitHub Pages AAAA records 2606:50c0:8000::153, 2606:50c0:8001::153, 2606:50c0:8002::153, 2606:50c0:8003::153; add www CNAME to matthiasroder.github.io; after propagation enable/enforce HTTPS in GitHub Pages.
close_reason: Audit documented repo Pages config, DNS records, and live IPv4/IPv6 HTTP behavior

title: Publish matters.global via GitHub Pages
description: Make the custom domain matters.global reliably point to the project's GitHub Pages website.
acceptance_criteria: matters.global resolves to the intended GitHub Pages site; HTTPS is valid; apex/www behavior is explicit and tested.
close_reason: matters.global is published through GitHub Pages with HTTPS enforced

title: Add shorthand matter creation CLI
description: Implement a human-friendly matters create command that can create one or more matters with inline conditions and dependency chains from a compact expression such as 'go to Mars (human lands and stays on Mars for at least one year) > build spaceship > assemble spaceship in earth orbit'.
close_reason: Implemented matters create shorthand with tests and README docs

title: Use personal matters graph as default state
description: The repository and matters skill currently prefer project-local .matters/matters.json when present. The desired default is /Users/matthias/.local/share/matters/matters.json unless --state or MATTERS_STATE explicitly overrides it.
close_reason: Implemented global personal graph as default state path; updated tests, README, and matters skill instructions.

title: Prototype Cytoscape matters graph renderer
description: Replace the current custom 3D force graph renderer with a Cytoscape.js-based graph view that better supports directed matter dependency readability while preserving existing web UI operations.
close_reason: Implemented Cytoscape renderer with adaptive Dagre/CoSE layouts, preserved web operations, updated docs/tests, verified with pytest and browser checks.

title: Declare pytest dev dependency or update local development docs
description: Deep review found README Local Development instructs contributors to run python -m pip install -e . followed by python -m pytest, but pyproject.toml declares runtime dependencies only and no optional/dependency-group test dependency. In a clean system Python this failed with No module named pytest. Add a test extra/dependency group or update the README command to install pytest explicitly, then verify a clean checkout can run the documented test command.
close_reason: Fixed. Added shared has_dependency_cycle engine helper, public merge cycle rejection, regression tests, pytest test extra, README install command, and verified with uv full suite plus a fresh pip editable install.

title: Validate dependency endpoints when loading and merging state
description: load_state converts dependencies to tuples without checking shape or endpoint membership, and merge_public_state indexes incoming dependency[0]/[1] directly. A state with dependency ['missing', 'a'] makes missing resolve as true and a appear actionable; a public merge dependency ['a'] raises IndexError instead of a user-facing validation error. Add state schema validation for dependency length and endpoints, and reject malformed public imports cleanly.
close_reason: Fixed with API mutation guards, serialized atomic state writes, portable terminal configuration, dependency validation, and regression tests.

title: Make the web terminal workspace portable
description: The terminal drawer defaults to Path('/Users/matthias/.openclaw/workspace') and /bin/zsh in package code, with no CLI option or documented override. That works only on this machine and makes the public package's terminal feature fail or expose the wrong workspace elsewhere. Default to the served project/current working directory or add explicit --terminal-workspace/--shell options and hide the terminal UI when unavailable.
close_reason: Fixed with API mutation guards, serialized atomic state writes, portable terminal configuration, dependency validation, and regression tests.

title: Document LLM extraction in README and matters skill
description: After the LLM extraction feature lands (matters_global-rxj), update README.md Extraction Proposals section and skills/matters/SKILL.md Extraction Workflow to describe the LLM engine, the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY requirement, --model/--no-llm flags, and the marker fallback behavior.
close_reason: Implemented LLM-based extraction engine (llm_extraction.py) with marker fallback, CLI/web wiring, anthropic dependency, offline tests (48 pass), README + SKILL docs. Independently validated: no critical/important findings.

title: Switch active graph from web UI
description: Why this exists: the running visualization window should be able to switch which matters graph/state file it displays without restarting the web server. What needs to be done: add a UI affordance and API support for changing the active graph state path, reload the visualization after switching, preserve existing graph interactions, and verify tests/browser behavior.
notes: Added a running-window graph switcher. Backend now uses a shared active state-path store and exposes POST /api/state to switch to another existing valid matters JSON file; all graph/edit/command endpoints read the current state path. Frontend adds a Graph section with a path input and Switch graph button, resets selection/search/status filter on switch, reloads selectors and graph data, and reports success/errors in the operation output. Verification: node --check src/matters/web_assets/app.js; uv run --with pytest python -m pytest (38 passed); git diff --check; browser switch from examples/matters.example.json to /tmp/matters-switch-graph.json in the same running window with state path, nodes, selectors, and canvas updated; mobile layout check confirmed switch form and canvas fit 390px viewport; console errors none. Status: ready to close. Blockers: none. Follow-ups: none.
close_reason: Completed running-window graph switching with backend state-path switching, UI control, tests, and browser verification.

title: Lighten and soften graph UI layout
description: Why this exists: the current web graph UI feels too heavy and not joyful. What needs to be done: refresh the layout styling, color, spacing, and control treatment so the app feels lighter and more inviting while preserving existing graph and editing workflows.
notes: Refreshed the local web graph UI so it feels lighter and less heavy: replaced black global buttons with soft surfaced controls, added status-colored toolbar accents, warmed the graph background and label treatment, softened panel borders/headings, added focus states, reduced side-panel width, and kept the hidden terminal drawer behavior intact. Verification: node --check src/matters/web_assets/app.js; uv run --with pytest python -m pytest (36 passed); git diff --check; browser desktop and mobile checks against http://127.0.0.1:8786 with canvas present, no console errors, terminal drawer hidden, mobile canvas fitting 390px viewport, and no toolbar button text overflow. Status: ready to close. Blockers: none. Follow-ups: none.
close_reason: Completed lighter, softer web graph UI refresh with desktop/mobile browser verification and passing automated checks.

title: Switch visualization to force-directed organic layout
description: Why this exists: the visualization system should use a force-directed organic layout instead of the current deterministic/static arrangement. What needs to be done: inspect the existing visualization implementation, replace or adapt the layout engine to force-directed positioning, preserve current interaction behavior, and verify the app still builds/tests.
notes: Changed the 3D web visualization from a tight compact force setup to an organic force-directed layout with stronger charge, variable link distances, collision separation, weak status drift, organic initial seeding, and position preservation across refreshes. Fixed responsive graph sizing by measuring the containing graph pane instead of the stale inline graph width. Also fixed hidden UI handling so the terminal drawer no longer consumes graph viewport space when hidden, and updated README/tests to describe the organic layout. Verification: node --check src/matters/web_assets/app.js; uv run --with pytest python -m pytest (36 passed); git diff --check; browser desktop and mobile screenshots against http://127.0.0.1:8786 with clean console logs, canvas present, WebGL fallback hidden, and non-background pixel samples in the graph pane. Status: ready to close. Blockers: none. Follow-ups: none.
close_reason: Completed: visualization now uses an organic force-directed layout, responsive canvas sizing is fixed, hidden drawer state is respected, docs/tests are updated, and automated plus browser verification passed.

title: Collapse graph edit controls
description: Why this exists: the side panel currently always shows create matter and dependency controls, which takes too much space. What needs to be done: hide those controls behind buttons/expandable sections while keeping inspector and commands accessible.
close_reason: Collapsed Create Matter and Dependencies into native disclosure sections, leaving inspector and chat visible.

title: Build initial creativity research matters graph
description: Use the tested extraction workflow to create the first reviewed matters graph for creativity research.
acceptance_criteria: Reviewed creativity-research matters, conditions, and dependencies are persisted to the chosen matters state file or exported for explicit acceptance.
notes: Exported initial creativity research graph proposal to examples/creativity_research/initial_graph_proposal.json. It contains 6 proposed matters, condition sets, and 6 dependencies, with requires_confirmation=true and persisted=false. It intentionally does not modify /Users/matthias/.local/share/matters/matters.json. Validation: jq empty passes for expected.json and initial_graph_proposal.json; pytest passes with 25 tests.
close_reason: Initial creativity research graph proposal exported for explicit acceptance
